The '''Sniper airspeeder''' was a [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]] employed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

It was very poorly defended, focusing more on speed and attack power. The sniper could reach speeds of up to six hundred kilometers per hour, as a result of the ion afterburner fitted just behind the cockpit.<ref name="Cadinth" />
{{Ship-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Sniper airspeeder was based on a concept for the "Imperial Sniper Fighter" vehicle that would have been part of a proposed but ultimately rejected extension of [[Kenner Products|Kenner's]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' toy line, ''[[The Epic Continues]]''.<ref name="Concept 1">{{WebCite|url=http://theswca.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=50978|text=1985-86 Line Extension Presentation Binder Page 34 - Imperial Sniper Fighter|work=The Star Wars Collectors Archive|archivedate=20160705182448}}</ref> It is different than the similarly-named toy that Kenner ''did'' produce, the [[Imperial Sniper]].<ref name="Kenner">{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: The Power of the Force (1985 toy line)|pack=Imperial Sniper Vehicle|altlink=http://theswca.com/images-toys/figuretoys/impsniper.html}}</ref> The speeder and other toy concepts were added to the ''[[Star Wars Legends|Legends]]'' continuity in "[[The Battle of Cadinth]]" story and roleplaying game article from [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 2|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 2]].<ref name="Cadinth" />
